# PostgreSQL Client Authentication Configuration File
# ===================================================
#
# Refer to the "Client Authentication" section in the PostgreSQL
# documentation for a complete description of this file.  A short
# synopsis follows.
#
# ----------------------
# Authentication Records
# ----------------------
#
# This file controls: which hosts are allowed to connect, how clients
# are authenticated, which PostgreSQL user names they can use, which
# databases they can access.  Records take one of these forms:
#
# local         DATABASE  USER  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
# host          DATABASE  USER  ADDRESS  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
# hostssl       DATABASE  USER  ADDRESS  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
# hostnossl     DATABASE  USER  ADDRESS  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
# hostgssenc    DATABASE  USER  ADDRESS  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
# hostnogssenc  DATABASE  USER  ADDRESS  METHOD  [OPTIONS]
#
# (The uppercase items must be replaced by actual values.)
#
# The first field is the connection type:
# - "local" is a Unix-domain socket
# - "host" is a TCP/IP socket (encrypted or not)
# - "hostssl" is a TCP/IP socket that is SSL-encrypted
# - "hostnossl" is a TCP/IP socket that is not SSL-encrypted
# - "hostgssenc" is a TCP/IP socket that is GSSAPI-encrypted
# - "hostnogssenc" is a TCP/IP socket that is not GSSAPI-encrypted
#
# DATABASE can be "all", "sameuser", "samerole", "replication", a
# database name, a regular expression (if it starts with a slash (/))
# or a comma-separated list thereof.  The "all" keyword does not match
# "replication".  Access to replication must be enabled in a separate
# record (see example below).
#
# USER can be "all", a user name, a group name prefixed with "+", a
# regular expression (if it starts with a slash (/)) or a comma-separated
# list thereof.  In both the DATABASE and USER fields you can also write
# a file name prefixed with "@" to include names from a separate file.
#
# ADDRESS specifies the set of hosts the record matches.  It can be a
# host name, or it is made up of an IP address and a CIDR mask that is
# an integer (between 0 and 32 (IPv4) or 128 (IPv6) inclusive) that
# specifies the number of significant bits in the mask.  A host name
# that starts with a dot (.) matches a suffix of the actual host name.
# Alternatively, you can write an IP address and netmask in separate
# columns to specify the set of hosts.  Instead of a CIDR-address, you
# can write "samehost" to match any of the server's own IP addresses,
# or "samenet" to match any address in any subnet that the server is
# directly connected to.
#
# METHOD can be "trust", "reject", "md5", "password", "scram-sha-256",
# "gss", "sspi", "ident", "peer", "pam", "ldap", "radius" or "cert".
# Note that "password" sends passwords in clear text; "md5" or
# "scram-sha-256" are preferred since they send encrypted passwords.
#
# OPTIONS are a set of options for the authentication in the format
# NAME=VALUE.  The available options depend on the different
# authentication methods -- refer to the "Client Authentication"
# section in the documentation for a list of which options are
# available for which authentication methods.
#
# Database and user names containing spaces, commas, quotes and other
# special characters must be quoted.  Quoting one of the keywords
# "all", "sameuser", "samerole" or "replication" makes the name lose
# its special character, and just match a database or username with
# that name.
#
# ---------------
# Include Records
# ---------------
#
# This file allows the inclusion of external files or directories holding
# more records, using the following keywords:
#
# include           FILE
# include_if_exists FILE
# include_dir       DIRECTORY
#
# FILE is the file name to include, and DIR is the directory name containing
# the file(s) to include.  Any file in a directory will be loaded if suffixed
# with ".conf".  The files of a directory are ordered by name.
# include_if_exists ignores missing files.  FILE and DIRECTORY can be
# specified as a relative or an absolute path, and can be double-quoted if
# they contain spaces.
#
# -------------
# Miscellaneous
# -------------
#
# This file is read on server startup and when the server receives a
# SIGHUP signal.  If you edit the file on a running system, you have to
# SIGHUP the server for the changes to take effect, run "pg_ctl reload",
# or execute "SELECT pg_reload_conf()".
#
# ----------------------------------
# Put your actual configuration here
# ----------------------------------
#
# If you want to allow non-local connections, you need to add more
# "host" records.  In that case you will also need to make PostgreSQL
# listen on a non-local interface via the listen_addresses
# configuration parameter, or via the -i or -h command line switches.

# CAUTION: Configuring the system for local "trust" authentication
# allows any local user to connect as any PostgreSQL user, including
# the database superuser.  If you do not trust all your local users,
# use another authentication method.


# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# "local" is for Unix domain socket connections only
local   all             all                                     trust
# IPv4 local connections:
host    all             all             127.0.0.1/32            trust
# IPv6 local connections:
host    all             all             ::1/128                 trust
# Allow replication connections from localhost, by a user with the
# replication privilege.
local   replication     all                                     trust
host    replication     all             127.0.0.1/32            trust
host    replication     all             ::1/128                 trust
host    all             all             192.168.200.3/32        md5
host    all             all             192.168.200.0/32        md5
host    all             all             192.168.230.0/24        md5

host    pmsmis     sales_user   0.0.0.0/0     md5

to do same as below script

0 23 * * * /oracle/scripts/rman_incr0_backup2.sh >> /tmp/rman_incr0_backup2.log 2>&1 && /oracle/scripts/mv_backup_to_nas.sh
[oracle@mfundproddb ~]$
[oracle@mfundproddb ~]$ cat /oracle/scripts/mv_backup_to_nas.sh
#!/bin/bash

# ==========================================
# Variables
# ==========================================
NAS_SERVER="//192.168.201.5/DB Backups/MFund"
MOUNT_POINT="/mnt/nas_storage"
CRED_FILE="/root/nas_creds"
BACKUP_DIR="/oracle/backup/"
LOG_FILE="/tmp/mv_backup_to_nas.log"

# ==========================================
# Start Logging
# ==========================================
exec > >(tee -a "$LOG_FILE") 2>&1
echo "--------------------------------------------------"
echo "Script started at: $(date)"

# ==========================================
# 1. Ensure Mount Point Exists
# ==========================================
mkdir -p "$MOUNT_POINT"

# ==========================================
# 2. Mount the NAS (Using sudo with UID/GID for Oracle)
# ==========================================
echo "Mounting NAS..."
sudo mount -t cifs "$NAS_SERVER" "$MOUNT_POINT" -o credentials=$CRED_FILE,vers=3.0,uid=54321,gid=54321

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to mount NAS. Exiting."
    exit 1
fi
echo "NAS mounted successfully."

# ==========================================
# 3. Find and Move the Latest Backup (Tar and Log)
# ==========================================
# Get today's date in the same format as your backup files (DD_MM_YYYY)
TODAY=$(date +"%d_%m_%Y")

# Count how many files match today's date
FILE_COUNT=$(ls -1d "${BACKUP_DIR}"*"${TODAY}"* 2>/dev/null | wc -l)

if [ "$FILE_COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: No backup found for today ($TODAY) in $BACKUP_DIR. Unmounting and exiting."
    sudo umount "$MOUNT_POINT"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Found $FILE_COUNT file(s) matching today's date ($TODAY)."
echo "Moving files to NAS..."

# Move ALL files from today to the NAS
mv "${BACKUP_DIR}"*"${TODAY}"* "$MOUNT_POINT/"

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to move backup files. Unmounting and exiting."
    sudo umount "$MOUNT_POINT"
    exit 1
fi
echo "Backup files moved successfully."

# ==========================================
# 4. Unmount the NAS (Using sudo)
# ==========================================
echo "Unmounting NAS..."
sudo umount "$MOUNT_POINT"

if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "WARNING: Failed to unmount NAS. Please check manually: sudo umount $MOUNT_POINT"
else
    echo "NAS unmounted successfully."
fi

echo "Script completed at: $(date)"
echo "--------------------------------------------------"

[oracle@ backup]$ cat /oracle/scripts/rman_incr0_backup2.sh #!/bin/bash # Oracle RMAN backup with HTML report and per-run archive # ---- Oracle environment ---- export ORACLE_SID=MFPROD # <--- change if needed export ORACLE_HOME=/u01/app/oracle/product/19.0.0/dbhome_1 export PATH=$ORACLE_HOME/bin:$PATH # ---- Paths ---- BACKUP_ROOT="/oracle/backup" # <--- your destination TIMESTAMP="$(date +'%d_%m_%Y_%H_%M_%S')" # matches your MySQL style RUN_DIR="$BACKUP_ROOT/$TIMESTAMP" LOG_FILE="$BACKUP_ROOT/backup_logs_${TIMESTAMP}.txt" RMAN_LOG="$RUN_DIR/rman_${TIMESTAMP}.log" HTML_REPORT="$RUN_DIR/backup_report.html" ARCHIVE_NAME="$BACKUP_ROOT/oracle_bkp_${TIMESTAMP}.tar.gz" # Email recipients (optional) EMAILS=("kedar.bhingarde@abakkusinvest.com" "p.rambabu@abakkusinvest.com") mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR" # ---- Collect system info ---- IP=$(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}') DB_NAME="$ORACLE_SID" DISK_BEFORE=$(df -h "$BACKUP_ROOT" | awk 'NR==2 {print $4}') START_EPOCH=$(date +%s) # ---- Log: start ---- echo "RMAN backup started at $(date +'%d-%m-%Y %H:%M:%S')" >> "$LOG_FILE" # ---- RMAN backup ---- rman target / < "$RMAN_LOG" 2>&1 RUN { ALLOCATE CHANNEL c1 DEVICE TYPE DISK; BACKUP AS COMPRESSED BACKUPSET INCREMENTAL LEVEL 0 DATABASE FORMAT "$RUN_DIR/incr0_%d_%T_%U.bkp"; BACKUP AS COMPRESSED BACKUPSET CURRENT CONTROLFILE FORMAT "$RUN_DIR/ctrl_%d_%T_%U.bkp"; BACKUP AS COMPRESSED BACKUPSET SPFILE FORMAT "$RUN_DIR/spfile_%d_%T_%U.bkp"; BACKUP AS COMPRESSED BACKUPSET ARCHIVELOG ALL FORMAT "$RUN_DIR/arch_%d_%T_%U.bkp" DELETE INPUT; RELEASE CHANNEL c1; } EXIT; EOF # ---- Determine status ---- STATUS="SUCCESS" if grep -q "RMAN-00569: =============== ERROR MESSAGE STACK FOLLOWS ==============" "$RMAN_LOG"; then STATUS="FAILED" fi END_EPOCH=$(date +%s) DURATION=$((END_EPOCH - START_EPOCH)) DURATION_FMT=$(printf '%02dh:%02dm:%02ds' $((DURATION/3600)) $((DURATION%3600/60)) $((DURATION%60))) DISK_AFTER=$(df -h "$BACKUP_ROOT" | awk 'NR==2 {print $4}') # ---- Latest L0 backup file info ---- LATEST_FILE=$(ls -1t "$RUN_DIR"/incr0_*.bkp 2>/dev/null | head -n 1) if [ -n "$LATEST_FILE" ]; then SIZE=$(du -h "$LATEST_FILE" | cut -f1) MODIFIED_TIME=$(stat -c '%y' "$LATEST_FILE" | cut -d'.' -f1) BACKUP_INFO_ROW="$MODIFIED_TIMEIncremental Level 0$SIZE$LATEST_FILE" else BACKUP_INFO_ROW="No Incremental Level 0 backup file found" fi # ---- Error tail (if any) ---- if [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ]; then ERROR_MSG=$(grep -E "RMAN-|ORA-" "$RMAN_LOG" | tail -n 20) else ERROR_MSG="None" fi # ---- Generate HTML report ---- cat > "$HTML_REPORT" <

RMAN Backup Report

IP Address$IP
Database$DB_NAME
Status$STATUS
Start Time$(date -d @$START_EPOCH '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
End Time$(date -d @$END_EPOCH '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
Duration$DURATION_FMT
Disk Space Before$DISK_BEFORE
Disk Space After$DISK_AFTER
Backup Folder$RUN_DIR
Archive Path$ARCHIVE_NAME

Latest Incremental Level 0 Backup File

$BACKUP_INFO_ROW
Last ModifiedBackup TypeBackup SizeBackup File

Error Messages (if any)

$ERROR_MSG

file://$ARCHIVE_NAMEDownload archive | file://$RMAN_LOGView RMAN log | file://$RUN_DIROpen run folder

HTML # ---- Create single archive for this run ---- tar -czf "$ARCHIVE_NAME" -C "$RUN_DIR" . # ---- Email HTML report (optional) ---- if command -v /bin/s-nail >/dev/null 2>&1; then /bin/s-nail -s "RMAN Backup Report - $DB_NAME" -M "text/html" "${EMAILS[@]}" < "$HTML_REPORT" fi # ---- Log: finish ---- echo "RMAN backup finished at $(date +'%d-%m-%Y %H:%M:%S')" >> "$LOG_FILE" echo "Status : $STATUS" >> "$LOG_FILE" echo "Run folder : $RUN_DIR" >> "$LOG_FILE" echo "Archive created : $ARCHIVE_NAME" >> "$LOG_FILE" echo "RMAN log : $RMAN_LOG" >> "$LOG_FILE" echo "HTML report : $HTML_REPORT" >> "$LOG_FILE" echo "*****************" >> "$LOG_FILE" # Exit non-zero if failed (useful for cron monitoring) [ "$STATUS" = "SUCCESS" ] || exit 1 exit 0 dont change my anything just add password to zip and what we are getting folder also ll total 1052452 drwxr-xr-x. 2 oracle oinstall 4096 Apr 5 23:02 05_04_2026_23_00_01 -rw-r--r--. 1 oracle oinstall 414 Apr 4 23:03 backup_logs_05_04_2026_23_00_01.txt -rw-r--r--. 1 oracle oinstall 1077695106 Apr 5 23:03 oracle_bkp_05_04_2026_23_00_01.tar.gz like i just want backup log and tar.gz (with password) and dont forget that we have mail system also but i dont want that folder there without password and we are zipping it thhen whay we need that
